title: hymenoxys pumila
intro: Hymenoxys pumila, also known as Gray's goldeneye, is a perennial plant that belongs to the daisy family (Asteraceae) and is native to North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Hymenoxys pumila include its ability to treat fever, sore throats, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. Herbal preparations of Hymenoxys pumila can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, allowing for a variety of applications in traditional medicine.
